data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-12T14:27:02.693Z</current_datetime>

Help me write MSTest tests for this formatting utility. I want to use the right MSTest string assertion methods like Assert.StartsWith and Assert.EndsWith to validate the output formats.

```csharp
namespace Contoso.Text;

public sealed class StringFormatter
{
    public string FormatPhoneNumber(string digits)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(digits);
        if (digits.Length != 10)
            throw new ArgumentException("Must be exactly 10 digits.", nameof(digits));
        return $"({digits[..3]}) {digits[3..6]}-{digits[6..]}";
    }

    public string BuildGreeting(string name, string title = "Mr.")
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(name);
        return $"Dear {title} {name},";
    }

    public string GenerateSlug(string title)
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(title);
        return Regex.Replace(title.Trim().ToLowerInvariant(), @"[^a-z0-9]+", "-").Trim('-');
    }

    public string FormatCurrency(decimal amount, string symbol = "$")
    {
        return $"{symbol}{amount:F2}";
    }
}
```

data.content:
Quick note: MSTest doesn't have `Assert.StartsWith`/`Assert.EndsWith`. The string-specific assertions live on the **`StringAssert`** class — `StringAssert.StartsWith()`, `StringAssert.EndsWith()`, `StringAssert.Contains()`, `StringAssert.Matches()`.

Let me set up the project and write the tests.
data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to write MSTest tests for a `StringFormatter` class. They specifically mention using `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` methods. Let me think about this carefully.

In MSTest, the standard assertion methods are on the `Assert` class. However, `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` are NOT standard MSTest methods. The standard MSTest `Assert` class has methods like `Assert.AreEqual`, `Assert.IsTrue`, `Assert.IsNotNull`, etc., but not `Assert.StartsWith` or `Assert.EndsWith`.

In MSTest, string-specific assertions are typically done through `StringAssert` class:


- `StringAssert.StartsWith(string, substring)`
- `StringAssert.EndsWith(string, substring)`
- `StringAssert.Contains(string, substring)`
- `StringAssert.Matches(string, regex)`

So the user is asking for methods that don't exist in MSTest. I should clarify this and use the correct `StringAssert` methods instead. I'll also need to include `using System.Text.RegularExpressions;` for any regex operations. Now I'm ready to write comprehensive tests for the `StringFormatter` class.

Writing test methods...

data.content: 27 passed, 0 warnings. Here's the final test file:

All 27 tests pass with 0 warnings. Key correction from your request: MSTest's string assertions live on **`StringAssert`**, not `Assert`. The tests demonstrate all four methods:

| Method | Example use |
|---|---|
| `StringAssert.StartsWith` | Area code prefix, currency symbol |
| `StringAssert.EndsWith` | Last 4 digits, trailing comma, `.00` |
| `StringAssert.Contains` | Dash separator, name in greeting |
| `StringAssert.Matches` | Phone pattern, slug format, currency format |
| `StringAssert.DoesNotMatch` | Slug has no leading/trailing dashes |
